*Information about this image and its acquisition is forthcoming.*

IC 1805, the Heart Nebula, is a huge emission nebula whose glowing clouds of ionized hydrogen form a heart-shaped outline, in the constellation Cassiopeia. It lies about 7,500 light-years away, shines around magnitude 6.5, and spans an enormous 150 by 150 arcminutes (roughly 2.5 degrees) across the sky.

*This description was generated by an AI assistant from general astronomical references, not from Jay's own notes on this image — treat it as a starting point, not a verified caption.*
